assertions.text: Output contains at least 2 separate markdown tables (not everything crammed into one table)
assertions.evidence: Two markdown tables produced: 'Table 1: Plan Pricing' and 'Table 2: Cancellation and Refund'

assertions.text: The tables cover distinct concerns — e.g. pricing/plans is separate from cancellation/refund rules
assertions.evidence: Table 1 covers plan pricing (Monthly, Annual, Trial). Table 2 covers cancellation and refund calculation including the 24-hour exception. Different input columns confirm different concerns.

assertions.text: At least one output column in each table ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Table 1 has 'Price?' column. Table 2 has 'Refund?' column. Both end with '?'.

assertions.text: The 24-hour no-refund exception is included as a distinct scenario row
assertions.evidence: Row in Table 2: 'Cancel within 24h of renewal — no refund | Monthly | 0 | yes | 30 | £0.00' — distinct row with Renewal Just Charged? = yes

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'boolean', 'int', 'isProrated', camelCase identifiers
assertions.evidence: Column names used: Plan, Billing Cycle, Price?, Days Into Cycle, Renewal Just Charged?, Days in Cycle, Refund? — all domain language, no camelCase or type names
